ucs.title: [LLM] Rhysida (Vanilla Tempest) — ntdsutil IFM dump → PsExec lateral ransomware staging
ucs.description: Detects Rhysida's signature post-compromise chain: ntdsutil 'create full' / 'IFM' dump of NTDS.dit followed within 30 minutes by PsExec/PAExec/CSEXEC lateral execution by the same operator account. Microsoft tracks the Rhysida-deploying actor as Vanilla Tempest (formerly Storm-0832 / DEV-0832), historically overlapping with Vice Society. CISA #StopRansomware advisory AA23-319A explicitly calls out ntdsutil for credential theft and PsExec for ransomware deployment as the canonical Rhysida sequence.
ucs.splunk: | tstats `security_content_summariesonly` count min(_time) as ntds_time values(Processes.process) as ntds_cmd from datamodel=Endpoint.Processes where Processes.process_name=ntdsutil.exe AND (Processes.process="*ifm*" OR Processes.process="*create full*" OR Processes.process="*ac i ntds*" OR Processes.process="*activate instance ntds*") by Processes.user Processes.dest | rename Processes.* AS * | rename dest as ntds_dest | join type=inner user [ | tstats `security_content_summariesonly` count min(_time) as lateral_time values(Processes.process) as lateral_cmd values(Processes.dest) as lateral_dest from datamodel=Endpoint.Processes where Processes.process_name IN (psexec.exe,psexec64.exe,paexec.exe,csexec.exe,remcom.exe) OR (Processes.process_name=cmd.exe AND Processes.process="*\\\\*\\admin$*") by Processes.user | rename Processes.* AS * ] | eval delta_min=round((lateral_time-ntds_time)/60,1) | where delta_min>=0 AND delta_min<=30 | table user ntds_dest ntds_time ntds_cmd lateral_dest lateral_time lateral_cmd delta_min
ucs.kql:
let LookbackDays = 7d;
let WindowMin = 30m;
let PsExecBins = dynamic(["psexec.exe","psexec64.exe","paexec.exe","csexec.exe","remcom.exe"]);
let NtdsDump =
    DeviceProcessEvents
    | where Timestamp > ago(LookbackDays)
    | where FileName =~ "ntdsutil.exe"
    | where ProcessCommandLine has_any ("ifm","create full","ac i ntds","activate instance ntds")
    | where AccountName !endswith "$"
    | project NtdsTime = Timestamp, DcDevice = DeviceName, AccountSid, AccountName,
              NtdsCmd = ProcessCommandLine;
DeviceProcessEvents
| where Timestamp > ago(LookbackDays)
| where FileName in~ (PsExecBins)
   or (FileName =~ "cmd.exe" and ProcessCommandLine has_any (@"\\admin$", @"\c$") and ProcessCommandLine has_any ("start","copy","sc create","sc \\\\"))
| where AccountName !endswith "$"
| join kind=inner NtdsDump on $left.AccountSid == $right.AccountSid
| where Timestamp between (NtdsTime .. NtdsTime + WindowMin)
| project NtdsTime, DcDevice, NtdsCmd,
          LateralTime = Timestamp, LateralDevice = DeviceName,
          AccountName, FileName, ProcessCommandLine,
          DelaySec = datetime_diff('second', Timestamp, NtdsTime),
          IsRemoteSession = InitiatingProcessAccountName
| order by NtdsTime asc
ucs.sentinel_kql:
let LookbackDays = 7d;
let WindowMin = 30m;
let PsExecBins = dynamic(["psexec.exe","psexec64.exe","paexec.exe","csexec.exe","remcom.exe"]);
let NtdsDump =
    SecurityEvent
    | where TimeGenerated > ago(LookbackDays)
    | where EventID == 4688
    | where NewProcessName endswith @"\ntdsutil.exe"
    | where CommandLine has_any ("ifm","create full","ac i ntds","activate instance ntds")
    | where SubjectUserName !endswith "$"
    | project NtdsTime = TimeGenerated, DcDevice = Computer,
              SubjectUserSid, AccountName = SubjectUserName, NtdsCmd = CommandLine;
SecurityEvent
| where TimeGenerated > ago(LookbackDays)
| where EventID == 4688
| where SubjectUserName !endswith "$"
| extend BinName = tolower(tostring(split(NewProcessName, @"\")[-1]))
| where BinName in (PsExecBins)
   or (BinName == "cmd.exe" and CommandLine has_any (@"\admin$", @"\c$") and CommandLine has_any ("start","copy","sc create"))
| join kind=inner NtdsDump on $left.SubjectUserSid == $right.SubjectUserSid
| where TimeGenerated between (NtdsTime .. NtdsTime + WindowMin)
| project NtdsTime, DcDevice, NtdsCmd,
          LateralTime = TimeGenerated, LateralDevice = Computer,
          AccountName, BinName, CommandLine,
          DelaySec = datetime_diff('second', TimeGenerated, NtdsTime)
| order by NtdsTime asc
ucs.rationale: Rhysida operators are documented (CISA AA23-319A, Sophos X-Ops, Microsoft Threat Intel on Vanilla Tempest) to specifically use the ntdsutil 'IFM' database snapshot trick to extract NTDS.dit on a domain controller — this exact sub-command is rare outside of legitimate AD recovery and the Rhysida playbook. Pairing the IFM dump with same-account PsExec/PAExec/CSEXEC execution within 30 minutes captur

ucs.title: [LLM] Rhysida ransomware impact — '.rhysida' extension rename burst + CriticalBreachDetected.pdf ransom note drop
ucs.description: Catches the encryption-stage signature of the Rhysida cryptor: high-velocity file renames to the '.rhysida' extension and creation of the actor's distinctive 'CriticalBreachDetected.pdf' ransom note across multiple directories on a host. Both artifacts are unique to Rhysida (Vanilla Tempest / Storm-0832) and not shared with other ransomware families, making this a near-zero-FP last-line detection for hosts that slipped through earlier kill-chain alerts.
ucs.splunk: | tstats `security_content_summariesonly` count min(_time) as first_seen max(_time) as last_seen values(Filesystem.file_path) as paths values(Filesystem.process_name) as processes dc(Filesystem.file_name) as unique_files from datamodel=Endpoint.Filesystem where (Filesystem.action IN ("created","renamed","modified")) AND (Filesystem.file_name="*.rhysida" OR Filesystem.file_name="CriticalBreachDetected.pdf") by Filesystem.dest Filesystem.user _time span=5m | rename Filesystem.* AS * | where count>=5 OR (count>=1 AND match(mvjoin(paths,","),"CriticalBreachDetected\.pdf")) | eval severity="critical", actor="Rhysida / Vanilla Tempest" | table _time dest user count unique_files paths processes severity actor
ucs.kql:
DeviceFileEvents
| where Timestamp > ago(7d)
| where (ActionType in ("FileRenamed","FileCreated","FileModified") and FileName endswith ".rhysida")
    or (ActionType == "FileCreated" and FileName =~ "CriticalBreachDetected.pdf")
| summarize EncryptedFileCount = countif(FileName endswith ".rhysida"),
            RansomNoteDrops    = countif(FileName =~ "CriticalBreachDetected.pdf"),
            FirstSeen          = min(Timestamp),
            LastSeen           = max(Timestamp),
            DistinctFolders    = dcount(FolderPath),
            SampleParents      = make_set(InitiatingProcessFileName, 10),
            SampleParentCmds   = make_set(InitiatingProcessCommandLine, 10),
            SamplePaths        = make_set(FolderPath, 15),
            ActingUser         = make_set(InitiatingProcessAccountName, 5)
            by DeviceId, DeviceName, bin(Timestamp, 5m)
| where EncryptedFileCount >= 5 or RansomNoteDrops >= 1
| order by FirstSeen desc
ucs.sentinel_kql:
ImFileEvent
| where TimeGenerated > ago(7d)
| where (EventType in ("FileRenamed","FileCreated","FileModified") and TargetFileName endswith ".rhysida")
    or (EventType == "FileCreated" and TargetFileName =~ "CriticalBreachDetected.pdf")
| summarize EncryptedFileCount = countif(TargetFileName endswith ".rhysida"),
            RansomNoteDrops    = countif(TargetFileName =~ "CriticalBreachDetected.pdf"),
            FirstSeen          = min(TimeGenerated),
            LastSeen           = max(TimeGenerated),
            DistinctFolders    = dcount(TargetFilePath),
            SampleParents      = make_set(ActingProcessName, 10),
            SampleParentCmds   = make_set(ActingProcessCommandLine, 10),
            SamplePaths        = make_set(TargetFilePath, 15),
            ActingUser         = make_set(ActorUsername, 5)
            by DvcHostname, bin(TimeGenerated, 5m)
| where EncryptedFileCount >= 5 or RansomNoteDrops >= 1
| order by FirstSeen desc
ucs.sigma_yaml:
title: Rhysida Ransomware Encryption Artifacts (.rhysida extension and CriticalBreachDetected.pdf)
id: 5b9d3f68-7a4c-4f1a-9c1e-rhysida0001
status: stable
description: Detects file-level artifacts unique to Rhysida ransomware (Vanilla Tempest / Storm-0832) — encrypted files renamed with the .rhysida extension and the actor's signature ransom note CriticalBreachDetected.pdf.
references:
  - https://www.cisa.gov/news-events/cybersecurity-advisories/aa23-319a
  - https://news.sophos.com/en-us/2023/11/14/connections-between-rhysida-and-vice-society
author: Detection Engineering
date: 2024/01/01
tags:
  - attack.impact
  - attack.t1486
  - attack.t1490
logsource:
  category: file_event
  product: windows
detection:
  selection_ext:
    TargetFilename|endswith: '.rhysida'
  selection_note:
    TargetFilename|endswith: '\CriticalBreachDetected.pdf'
  condition: 1 of selection_*
falsepositives:
  - Highly unlikely - both artifacts are exclusive to Rhysida ransomware tradecraft
level: critical
ucs.rationale: Both the '.rhysida' file extension and the 'CriticalBreachDetected.pdf' ransom note filename are exclusive identifiers of the Rhysida ransomware family — they appear in CISA AA23-319A, Sophos X-Ops reporting, and Microsoft's Vanilla Tempest profile, and have not been observed cross-pollinated into other ransomware brands. A 5-minute file-burst window combined with a multi-folder fan-out test catch
_sigma_issues: sigma parse error: Sigma rule identifier must be an UUID
